QF.id: IV Introduction to Interest Rate Derivatives_5

answer.text: Starts in 3 months and ends in 6 months from today
Starts in 6 months and ends in 3 more months

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** In FRA notation, the first number indicates when the contract starts (3 months from today), and the second number indicates when it ends (6 months from today). The actual borrowing/lending period is the difference: 6-3 = 3 months.
**The Trap:** A 3×6 does NOT mean 3+6=9 months total. The numbers reference the start and end dates from today, so the forward period is 3 months (months 3 to 6).
**Cross-Exam Context:** FRA notation is standard across all derivative markets and is tested in Series IV. Similar notation applies to interest rate swaps and forward starting swaps.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Common FRA designations: 1×4 (1-month start, 3-month period), 3×6 (3-month start, 3-month period), 6×12 (6-month start, 6-month period).
